ab: Hngkawas empusal kbkuy empusal dha siida, mneuda pprngaw ka klwaan tduwa burux powsa hangan kari ta nanaq ka patas hnyigan Seejiq Tnpusu.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: Smniling ku risaw alang Walis Huwac, hiya o bsiyaq balay mquri klwaan embiyax meysa patas hnyigan “ burux powsa hangan kari ta nanaq.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Walis Huwac。
en: I interviewed Walis Huwac, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: Mnhdu nami pprngaw , jiyax sngayan nii musa sapah na smiling hyaan.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: Saw mniq sniling siida, pgkla knan pnyahan hangan na, dsun ku na pgkla kingal ruwan sapah na ni alang na uri.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: Smiling ku hyaan, hmuya ma su ida mkray lnglungan priyux hangan, uda powsa patas seuxal o naqih kiya hug?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: Rngagan ku na, yaasa dmuuy kari Tlu o ini sleexan balay pgkla hanagan na, duma seejiq “瓦先生” msa mlawa hyaan uri, kiya ni saw bi smhulis.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「瓦先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Wa Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: Rmngaw ka Walis Huwac, “Hangan bnatas kari Seejiq Tnpusu o kika balay bi hangan ta. Hangan mu o ririh saw pgkla klgan seejiq ta, tnsapah ta, hnyigan ta, tay saw emphbaraw balay ka seejiq dhuq hmlay hangan dha nanaq.”
ch: Walis Huwac說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Walis Huwac said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
